TEST_CASE: Users table has correct columns and constraints
DETAILS: users table has: id(uuid), auth_id(uuid), full_name, email(unique), phone, role(check: farmer/owner/admin), profile_image_url, address, city, state, pincode, aadhar_number, is_verified, is_active, created_at, updated_at. FK to auth.users present.

vehicles table has 25 columns including owner_id(FK->users), pricing fields, location fields, is_approved, is_available, fuel_type, horsepower, capacity, total_bookings, average_rating. All necessary fields present.

bookings table has all necessary fields: vehicle_id, farmer_id, owner_id (all FK), date/time fields, pricing breakdown (subtotal/tax/total), status(check constraint), payment_status(check constraint), booking_notes, cancellation_reason.
reviews table has: booking_id(unique FK), vehicle_id(FK), farmer_id(FK), rating(check: 1-5), review_text, owner_reply, is_visible. Prevents duplicate reviews per booking via UNIQUE constraint.
payments table has: booking_id(FK), farmer_id(FK), owner_id(FK), amount, payment_method, transaction_id, payment_gateway, status(check: pending/success/failed/refunded), payment_date.
notifications table has: user_id(FK), title, message, type, is_read(default false), related_id(nullable uuid), created_at. Minimal but functional.

ALL 6 tables (users, vehicles, bookings, reviews, payments, notifications) have RLS DISABLED. Any user with the anon key can read or modify any row in any table. This is a critical security vulnerability.
RECOMMENDATION: IMMEDIATELY enable RLS on all tables and create proper policies per role (farmer/owner/admin). See Supabase docs: https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security

DETAILS: DB has 4 users: 1 admin, 1 owner, 2 farmers. Demo credentials are populated and app is seeded for testing.
2 vehicles exist in DB, both is_approved=true and is_available=true. Vehicles are ready for farmer browsing.
3 bookings in DB: 2 pending, 1 cancelled. Booking lifecycle data is present.
3 payment records exist, all with status=success. Payment records are linked correctly to bookings.
4 notifications exist, all unread (is_read=false). Notifications are being created but not marked as read automatically.
RECOMMENDATION: Consider adding a read-all on screen open as UX improvement

DETAILS: 0 reviews exist in DB. Review system exists in schema and code but has no data. This may indicate review submission hasn't been tested end-to-end.
RECOMMENDATION: Test the review submission flow after completing a booking lifecycle

DETAILS: SupabaseService singleton wraps Supabase.initialize() with correct URL and publishable key. signUp, signIn, signOut, resetPassword all delegate directly to supabase_flutter client.
createUserProfile() inserts into public.users with auth_id, full_name, email, phone, role, city, state, is_verified=true, is_active=true. Auth and profile are correctly linked.
getCurrentUserProfile() queries users table by auth_id=currentUser.id using maybeSingle(). Gracefully returns null if not found.
getApprovedVehicles() filters by is_approved=true AND is_available=true, with optional vehicleType, city, state, maxPrice filters. Client-side search query applied as final filter.
RECOMMENDATION: Consider moving text search to DB-side (PostgREST full-text search) for performance at scale

DETAILS: getVehicleById() uses maybeSingle() correctly. Returns null if vehicle not found, no exception thrown.
getOwnerVehicles(), addVehicle(), updateVehicle(), deleteVehicle(), toggleAvailability(), approveVehicle() all correctly implemented.
createBooking() inserts and returns generated booking id using .select('id').single(). Correct pattern.
isVehicleAvailable() checks for overlapping confirmed/active bookings using lt(end_date) and gt(start_date) which correctly implements interval overlap detection.
updateBookingStatus(), updatePaymentStatus(), cancelBooking() all correctly update specific fields by ID.
getOwnerEarnings() fetches paid bookings, calculates total and this-month earnings. Logic is correct.
createPayment() correctly sets payment_gateway=offline for Cash, online for others. Sets status=pending for Cash, success for digital. Generates TXN ID for digital payments.
RECOMMENDATION: WARN: No real payment gateway integrated. Online payments are marked success without actual gateway verification.

DETAILS: getPaymentByBooking(), getFarmerPayments(), getOwnerPayments() all correctly implemented with proper ordering.
sendNotification() inserts into notifications table with userId, title, message, type, is_read=false, related_id.

notifyOwnerNewBooking(), notifyFarmerBookingConfirmed(), notifyFarmerBookingRejected(), notifyFarmerBookingActive(), notifyFarmerBookingCompleted(), notifyOwnerBookingCancelled(), notifyPaymentReceived() all properly implemented.
subscribeToNotifications() emits a dummy NotificationModel with empty id/title/message when rows is empty. This is a code smell - consumers must filter empty notifications.
RECOMMENDATION: Return an empty stream or use distinct() filtering instead of emitting dummy records

DETAILS: addReview() inserts with booking_id, vehicle_id, farmer_id, rating, review_text. No average_rating update on vehicles table is performed after review submission.
RECOMMENDATION: Add a DB trigger or post-insert logic to update vehicles.average_rating after a new review

Supabase URL and publishable anon key are hardcoded in lib/utils/constants.dart (committed to source). The supabaseAnonKey is the new publishable format (sb_publishable_...) which is safer than JWT anon keys but still should not be in source control.
RECOMMENDATION: Move credentials to .env file or use flutter_dotenv package. Add .env to .gitignore. Use build-time environment variables.

TEST_CASE: No RLS policies protect sensitive data
DETAILS: All 6 tables have RLS disabled. Any caller with the anon key can: read all users' phone/aadhar numbers, read all bookings, read all payments, delete any vehicle. This is a critical vulnerability for a production app.
RECOMMENDATION: Enable RLS with policies: users can only read/update their own profile; farmers see own bookings; owners see own vehicles+bookings; admin sees all.

DETAILS: Online payments (UPI/Card/Net Banking) are simulated. Transaction IDs are generated locally (TXN + timestamp). No actual payment verification occurs. Payments marked 'success' without gateway confirmation.
RECOMMENDATION: Integrate Razorpay/PayU/Stripe. Verify payment server-side before marking booking payment_status=paid.

DETAILS: Password validation only checks minimum 6 characters. No complexity requirements (uppercase/number/special char). Weak passwords allowed.
RECOMMENDATION: Add password complexity requirements: min 8 chars, uppercase, number, special character.

DETAILS: Email validation only checks for @ character presence. Invalid emails like test@ or @domain.com would pass validation.
RECOMMENDATION: Use RegExp for proper email validation: RegExp(r'^[^@]+@[^@]+\.[^@]+')

DETAILS: booking_history_screen.dart line 71: context.read<AuthProvider>() called after async await without mounted guard. Dart static analyzer reports this as a warning.
RECOMMENDATION: Capture user before the first await: 'final user = context.read<AuthProvider>().currentUser!;' at line 64 start, remove line 71.

DETAILS: SupabaseService uses singleton factory pattern. All other services (VehicleService, BookingService, etc.) instantiate SupabaseService() which correctly returns the singleton.
AuthProvider extends ChangeNotifier. Used with Consumer<AuthProvider> and context.watch<>/context.read<> consistently throughout the app.
Clear separation: models (data), services (backend), providers (state), screens (UI), widgets (reusable UI components), utils (theme/constants/router). Well-structured architecture.
Based on conversation history, withOpacity() has been replaced with withValues(alpha:) throughout the codebase. Verified in reviewed files - only withValues() seen.
All async service calls in screens are wrapped in try-catch blocks. Errors display via SnackBar with the error message. mounted checks prevent setState on unmounted widgets.
Code uses null-aware operators (?., ??, ?.copyWith) consistently. VehicleModel and BookingModel use nullable types where appropriate. Dart null safety is leveraged.

Complete flow: farmer logs in -> views vehicles -> selects vehicle -> picks dates/time -> selects payment -> confirms -> booking created in DB -> payment record created -> owner notified. Verified by code analysis and DB state.
Owner can: log in -> view pending bookings -> confirm/reject -> mark as active -> mark as completed. Each step sends farmer notification. Verified by code analysis.
Admin can: log in -> view pending vehicles -> approve/reject. Approved vehicles become visible to farmers. Admin can also manage users.
Notifications created in DB for all booking lifecycle events. getUserNotifications(), markAsRead(), markAllAsRead() APIs exist. UI screens for notification display exist.
RECOMMENDATION: WARN: No push notifications (FCM/APNs). Notifications are DB-only, requiring app to be open.

DETAILS: ReviewService.addReview() exists and is correct. hasReview() prevents duplicates. However 0 reviews in DB suggests the review submission UI may not be prominently accessible or review flow not fully connected in vehicle_detail_screen.
RECOMMENDATION: Verify review submission button appears in vehicle_detail_screen for completed bookings. Add post-booking review prompt.

DETAILS: Each portal (farmer/owner) likely has bottom navigation based on screen structure. Owner has Home/Bookings/Earnings/Profile. Farmer has Home/Search/Bookings/Profile.
AppTheme defines consistent colors (primaryGreen, primaryGreenDark, accentAmber, errorRed, successGreen, skyBlue, greyText). Used consistently across all screens via constants.
All data-loading screens show CircularProgressIndicator while loading. Empty states with icons and messages shown when data is empty. Consistent UX pattern.
App uses ₹ symbol, Indian states list (28 states), Aadhar number field. GST tax rate (5%) applied. Context appropriate for Indian agricultural market.

App is functionally complete for demo/development. Core booking lifecycle works end-to-end. Critical blocker for production: RLS must be enabled, payment gateway must be integrated. Minor issues: 1 async context bug, forgot password not wired, review avg not updated.
RECOMMENDATION: PRODUCTION BLOCKERS: (1) Enable RLS on all 6 tables with proper policies. (2) Integrate real payment gateway. (3) Move credentials out of source code. MINOR FIXES: (4) Wire forgot password. (5) Fix async context bug in booking_history_screen.dart:71. (6) Add trigger to update vehicle average_rating on review insert.
